heading: Modify a Stream Processor
body:
You can modify the following elements of an existing stream processor:

- Name

- [Aggregation pipeline stages](atlas-sp-aggregation)

- [Dead Letter Queue](atlas-sp-dlq)

To modify a stream processor, do the following:

- [Stop the stream processor](streams-manage-stop).

- [Modify the stream processor](streams-manage-modify-steps).

- [Restart the stream processor](streams-manage-start).

By default, modified processors restore from the last checkpoint. Alternatively,
you can set `resumeFromCheckpoint=false`, in which case the processor only
retains summary stats. When you modify a processor with open windows, the windows
are entirely recomputed on the updated pipeline.

> **Note:** If you change the name of a stream processor for which you had
> [Configure Alert Settings](configure-alerts) the [Atlas Stream Processing Alerts](atlas-sp-alerts) alert by using an Operator
> (which contains matcher expressions like `is`, `contains`, and
> more), Atlas won't trigger alerts for the renamed stream
> processor if the matcher expression doesn't match the new name. To
> monitor the renamed stream processor, [Configure Alert Settings](configure-alerts) the alert.
